description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player mounted on a wooden stand with two shelves. The most prominent feature is a large, flared, green metal horn with a yellowish interior and decorative patterning, which serves as the sound amplifier. The main body of the player is a dark, boxy structure with a visible turntable platter holding a black vinyl record. The stand is made of dark wood, featuring a rectangular tabletop and a lower shelf with ornate inlay detailing. Metal supports connect the shelves, and the overall construction appears sturdy and classic. The object exudes an antique charm, reminiscent of early 20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large green horn, wooden stand, and visible vinyl record, exuding antique charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large green horn, the vinyl record on the platter, and the wooden stand with its decorative lower shelf.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length and curvature of the horn, the boxy player body, and the two-tiered wooden stand.
description_view_2: Direct view into the horn's opening, revealing its yellowish interior and decorative pattern, with the record and stand visible below.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's profile, the record, and the structure of the wooden stand.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and clearly visible, the turntable platter is circular and black, supporting a vinyl record. It is proportionally small compared to the overall record player, with a smooth, flat surface and a central spindle. The platter appears to be made of metal or hard plastic, with no visible grooves or ornate design elements.
attribute_tonearm: Present, the tonearm is a slender, curved component extending from the side of the player body toward the record. It appears to be made of metal with a dark finish and is positioned over the platter, suggesting it is ready to play the record. The tonearm has a visible joint near its base.
